revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、淀川北岸、桧尾川流域から茨木川流域にあり、[[大阪府]]...'
revision.text:
'''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、淀川北岸、桧尾川流域から茨木川流域にあり、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]と[[茨木市]]にかけて広がる古墳と遺跡の総称。
== 概要 == [[淀川]]右岸地域、[[北摂山地]]の山腹部から丘陵部にかけて広がり、古墳だけでも大小500基以上を数える。古代三島地方の中心地であったこの地には、古墳時代初期から終末期までの各時代を代表する古墳が含まれ、[[羽曳野市|羽曳野]]・[[藤井寺市|藤井寺]]周辺の[[古市古墳群]]、[[堺市|堺]]周辺の[[百舌鳥古墳群]]と並ぶ規模を誇る、日本有数の古墳地帯である。
== 主な古墳 == *[[安満宮山古墳]] :3世紀中頃、古墳時代初頭の古墳。三島古墳群最古。 *岡本山古墳 :3世紀後半、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島地方最初の王墓。全長約120ｍ、後円部径約70ｍの前方後円墳。 *弁天山古墳 :3世紀末〜4世紀初頭、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島の王の王墓。全長約100ｍ、後円部径約70ｍの前方後円墳。 *[[闘鶏山古墳]] 国の史跡 :4世紀前半、古墳時代前期の古墳。全長86.4ｍ、後円部径約60ｍの前方後円墳。 *郡家車塚古墳 :4世紀末、古墳時代前期の古墳。三島地方の王墓。全長が約85.6ｍ、後円部径約51.3ｍの前方後円墳。 *[[新池埴輪製作遺跡]] 国の史跡 :5世紀中頃〜6世紀中頃まで約100年間操業していた、日本最古最大級の埴輪生産遺跡。 *[[太田茶臼山古墳]] 継体天皇稜 :5世紀中頃〜後半、古墳時代中期の古墳。総長250m・総幅約150mの前方後円墳。 *[[今城塚古墳]] 国の史跡 :6世紀前半、古墳時代後期の古墳。総長約350m・総幅約340mの前方後円墳。淀川流域最大の墳墓。 *[[塚原古墳群]] :6世紀中頃〜7世紀中頃の古墳群。かつては15群・約110基を数えたが、現在では約40基が残る。塚原八十塚と呼ばれ、[[ウイリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に紹介され、古墳研究の先駆けとなった古墳群。 *塚脇古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の古墳群。約50基の古墳で構成されている。 *安満古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の群衆墳。40数基の横穴式石室をもつ古墳が確認されている。 *[[阿武山古墳]] 国の史跡 :7世紀末、古墳時代終末期の古墳。盛り土はなく、直径82mの円墳。[[藤原鎌足]]の墓とされる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:みしまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史‎]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史‎]]

{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月}} '''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、淀川北岸、桧尾川流域から茨木川流域にあり、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]と[[茨木市]]にかけて広がる古墳と遺跡の総称。

{{DEFAULTSORT:みしまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史‎]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史‎]]

== 主な古墳 == *[[安満宮山古墳]] :3世紀中頃、古墳時代初頭の古墳。三島古墳群最古。 *岡本山古墳 :3世紀後半、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島地方最初の王墓。全長約120m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *弁天山古墳 :3世紀末〜4世紀初頭、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島の王の王墓。全長約100m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *[[闘鶏山古墳]] 国の史跡 :4世紀前半、古墳時代前期の古墳。全長86.4m、後円部径約60mの前方後円墳。 *郡家車塚古墳 :4世紀末、古墳時代前期の古墳。三島地方の王墓。全長が約85.6m、後円部径約51.3mの前方後円墳。 *[[新池埴輪製作遺跡]] 国の史跡 :5世紀中頃〜6世紀中頃まで約100年間操業していた、日本最古最大級の埴輪生産遺跡。 *[[太田茶臼山古墳]] 継体天皇稜 :5世紀中頃〜後半、古墳時代中期の古墳。総長250m・総幅約150mの前方後円墳。 *[[今城塚古墳]] 国の史跡 :6世紀前半、古墳時代後期の古墳。総長約350m・総幅約340mの前方後円墳。淀川流域最大の墳墓。 *[[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|塚原古墳群]] :6世紀中頃〜7世紀中頃の古墳群。かつては15群・約110基を数えたが、現在では約40基が残る。塚原八十塚と呼ばれ、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に紹介され、古墳研究の先駆けとなった古墳群。 *塚脇古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の古墳群。約50基の古墳で構成されている。 *安満古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の群衆墳。40数基の横穴式石室をもつ古墳が確認されている。 *[[阿武山古墳]] 国の史跡 :7世紀末、古墳時代終末期の古墳。盛り土はなく、直径82mの円墳。[[藤原鎌足]]の墓とされる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:みしまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史‎]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史‎]] [[Category:古墳群]]

{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月}} '''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、[[淀川]]北岸、[[桧尾川]]流域から[[茨木川]]流域にあり、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]と[[茨木市]]（旧[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島郡]]）にかけて広がる[[古墳]]と遺跡の総称。
== 概要 == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|三島野古墳群と他の古墳群の位置関係]] [[淀川]]右岸地域、[[北摂山地]]の山腹部から丘陵部にかけて広がり、古墳だけでも大小500基以上を数える。古代三島地方の中心地であったこの地には、古墳時代初期から終末期までの各時代を代表する古墳が含まれ、[[羽曳野市|羽曳野]]・[[藤井寺市|藤井寺]]周辺の[[古市古墳群]]、[[堺市|堺]]周辺の[[百舌鳥古墳群]]と並ぶ規模を誇る、日本有数の古墳地帯である。
== 主な古墳 == *[[安満宮山古墳]] :3世紀中頃、古墳時代初頭の古墳。三島古墳群最古。 *岡本山古墳 :3世紀後半、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島地方最初の王墓。全長約120m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *弁天山古墳 :3世紀末〜4世紀初頭、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島の王の王墓。全長約100m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *[[闘鶏山古墳]] 国の史跡 :4世紀前半、古墳時代前期の古墳。全長86.4m、後円部径約60mの前方後円墳。 *[[郡家車塚古墳]] :4世紀末、古墳時代前期の古墳。三島地方の王墓。全長が約85.6m、後円部径約51.3mの前方後円墳。 *[[太田茶臼山古墳]] 継体天皇陵 :5世紀中頃〜後半、古墳時代中期の古墳。総長250m・総幅約150mの前方後円墳。 *[[今城塚古墳]] 国の史跡 :6世紀前半、古墳時代後期の古墳。総長約350m・総幅約340mの前方後円墳。淀川流域最大の墳墓。 *[[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|塚原古墳群]] :6世紀中頃〜7世紀中頃の古墳群。かつては15群・約110基を数えたが、現在では約40基が残る。塚原八十塚と呼ばれ、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に紹介され、古墳研究の先駆けとなった古墳群。 *塚脇古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の古墳群。約50基の古墳で構成されている。 *安満古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の群衆墳。40数基の横穴式石室をもつ古墳が確認されている。 *[[阿武山古墳]] 国の史跡 :7世紀末、古墳時代終末期の古墳。盛り土はなく、直径82mの円墳。[[藤原鎌足]]の墓とされる。 <gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[郡家車塚古墳]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[太田茶臼山古墳]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[今城塚古墳]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[阿武山古墳]] </gallery>

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]]

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs (**kofun**) and archaeological sites located along the **northern bank of the Yodo River**, spanning the **Hio River Basin** to the **Ibaraki River Basin** in **Takatsuki City** and **Ibaraki City**, Osaka Prefecture (formerly **Mishima District**).
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the **right bank of the Yodo River**, the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the **Hokusetsu Mountain Range** to its foothills and consists of over **500 kofun** of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative burial mounds from the **early to final periods of the Kofun era**. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near **Habikino** and **Fujiidera**, and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in **Sakai**, the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest burial mound clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * **Amanomiya Kofun** : A kofun from the **mid-3rd century**, marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* **Okamotoyama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun (**zenpō-kōen-fun**) measuring approximately **120 meters** in total length and **70 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Benten-yama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd to early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the **royal tomb** of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **100 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **70 meters**.
* **Tokeiyama Kofun** (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **86.4 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **60 meters**.
* **Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun** (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the **late 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately **85.6 meters** in total length and **51.3 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Ōta Chausuyama Kofun** (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the **mid-to-late 5th century**, a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **250 meters** and a total width of approximately **150 meters**.
* **Imashirozuka Kofun** (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 6th century**, a late Kofun period tomb. The **largest burial mound in the Yodo River basin**, measuring approximately **350 meters** in total length and **340 meters** in total width.
* **Tsukahara Kofun Group** (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the **mid-6th to mid-7th century**. Previously contained **15 subgroups** with **about 110 kofun**, but only **40 kofun** remain today. Known as **Tsukahara Hachijūzuka**, it was introduced to the world by **William Gowland**, becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* **Tsukawaki Kofun Group** : A kofun group from the **late 6th to 7th century**, consisting of approximately **50 kofun**.
* **Aman Kofun Group** : A **clustered kofun site** from the **late 6th to 7th century**. Over **40 kofun** with **yokoana-shiki sekishitsu** (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* **Abuyama Kofun** (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **late 7th century**, in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun (**empun**) with no mound, measuring **82 meters** in diameter. Thought to be the **tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari**.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs (**kofun**) and archaeological sites located along the **northern bank of the Yodo River**, spanning the **Hio River Basin** to the **Ibaraki River Basin** in **Takatsuki City** and **Ibaraki City**, Osaka Prefecture (formerly **Mishima District**).
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the **right bank of the Yodo River**, the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the **Hokusetsu Mountain Range** to its foothills and consists of over **500 kofun** of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative burial mounds from the **early to final periods of the Kofun era**. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near **Habikino** and **Fujiidera**, and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in **Sakai**, the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest burial mound clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * **Amanomiya Kofun** : A kofun from the **mid-3rd century**, marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* **Okamotoyama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun (**zenpō-kōen-fun**) measuring approximately **120 meters** in total length and **70 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Benten-yama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd to early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the **royal tomb** of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **100 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **70 meters**.
* **Tokeiyama Kofun** (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **86.4 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **60 meters**.
* **Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun** (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the **late 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately **85.6 meters** in total length and **51.3 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Ōta Chausuyama Kofun** (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the **mid-to-late 5th century**, a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **250 meters** and a total width of approximately **150 meters**.
* **Imashirozuka Kofun** (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 6th century**, a late Kofun period tomb. The **largest burial mound in the Yodo River basin**, measuring approximately **350 meters** in total length and **340 meters** in total width.
* **Tsukahara Kofun Group** (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the **mid-6th to mid-7th century**. Previously contained **15 subgroups** with **about 110 kofun**, but only **40 kofun** remain today. Known as **Tsukahara Hachijūzuka**, it was introduced to the world by **William Gowland**, becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* **Tsukawaki Kofun Group** : A kofun group from the **late 6th to 7th century**, consisting of approximately **50 kofun**.
* **Aman Kofun Group** : A **clustered kofun site** from the **late 6th to 7th century**. Over **40 kofun** with **yokoana-shiki sekishitsu** (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* **Abuyama Kofun** (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **late 7th century**, in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun (**empun**) with no mound, measuring **82 meters** in diameter. Thought to be the **tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari**.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Burial Mounds]] {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs (**kofun**) and archaeological sites located along the **northern bank of the Yodo River**, spanning the **Hio River Basin** to the **Ibaraki River Basin** in **Takatsuki City** and **Ibaraki City**, Osaka Prefecture (formerly **Mishima District**).
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the **right bank of the Yodo River**, the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the **Hokusetsu Mountain Range** to its foothills and consists of over **500 kofun** of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative burial mounds from the **early to final periods of the Kofun era**. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near **Habikino** and **Fujiidera**, and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in **Sakai**, the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest burial mound clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * **Amanomiya Kofun** : A kofun from the **mid-3rd century**, marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* **Okamotoyama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun (**zenpō-kōen-fun**) measuring approximately **120 meters** in total length and **70 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Benten-yama Kofun** : A kofun from the **late 3rd to early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the **royal tomb** of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **100 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **70 meters**.
* **Tokeiyama Kofun** (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **86.4 meters** and a rear mound diameter of **60 meters**.
* **Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun** (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the **late 4th century**, an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a **royal tomb** of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately **85.6 meters** in total length and **51.3 meters** in diameter at the rear mound.
* **Ōta Chausuyama Kofun** (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the **mid-to-late 5th century**, a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of **250 meters** and a total width of approximately **150 meters**.
* **Imashirozuka Kofun** (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **early 6th century**, a late Kofun period tomb. The **largest burial mound in the Yodo River basin**, measuring approximately **350 meters** in total length and **340 meters** in total width.
* **Tsukahara Kofun Group** (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the **mid-6th to mid-7th century**. Previously contained **15 subgroups** with **about 110 kofun**, but only **40 kofun** remain today. Known as **Tsukahara Hachijūzuka**, it was introduced to the world by **William Gowland**, becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* **Tsukawaki Kofun Group** : A kofun group from the **late 6th to 7th century**, consisting of approximately **50 kofun**.
* **Aman Kofun Group** : A **clustered kofun site** from the **late 6th to 7th century**. Over **40 kofun** with **yokoana-shiki sekishitsu** (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* **Abuyama Kofun** (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the **late 7th century**, in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun (**empun**) with no mound, measuring **82 meters** in diameter. Thought to be the **tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari**.

* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest burial mound in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative burial mounds from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest burial mound clusters in Japan.

* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.

* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest burial mound in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

[[ja:三島古墳群]]

A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.

Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

[[ja:%E4%B8%89%E5%B3%B6%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Mishima Kofuns]] {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

{{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:三島古墳群 up to rev 101897749
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月}} '''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、[[淀川]]北岸、[[桧尾川]]流域から[[茨木川]]流域にあり、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]と[[茨木市]]（旧[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島郡]]）にかけて広がる[[古墳]]と遺跡の総称。
== 概要 == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|三島野古墳群と他の古墳群の位置関係]] [[淀川]]右岸地域、[[北摂山地]]の山腹部から丘陵部にかけて広がり、古墳だけでも大小500基以上を数える。古代三島地方の中心地であったこの地には、古墳時代初期から終末期までの各時代を代表する古墳が含まれ、[[羽曳野市|羽曳野]]・[[藤井寺市|藤井寺]]周辺の[[古市古墳群]]、[[堺市|堺]]周辺の[[百舌鳥古墳群]]と並ぶ規模を誇る、日本有数の古墳地帯である。
== 主な古墳 == *[[安満宮山古墳]] :3世紀中頃、古墳時代初頭の古墳。三島古墳群最古。 *岡本山古墳 :3世紀後半、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島地方最初の王墓。全長約120m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *弁天山古墳 :3世紀末〜4世紀初頭、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島の王の王墓。全長約100m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *[[闘鶏山古墳]] 国の史跡 :4世紀前半、古墳時代前期の古墳。全長86.4m、後円部径約60mの前方後円墳。 *[[郡家車塚古墳]] :4世紀末、古墳時代前期の古墳。三島地方の王墓。全長が約85.6m、後円部径約51.3mの前方後円墳。 *[[太田茶臼山古墳]] 継体天皇陵 :5世紀中頃〜後半、古墳時代中期の古墳。総長250m・総幅約150mの前方後円墳。 *[[今城塚古墳]] 国の史跡 :6世紀前半、古墳時代後期の古墳。総長約350m・総幅約340mの前方後円墳。淀川流域最大の墳墓。 *[[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|塚原古墳群]] :6世紀中頃〜7世紀中頃の古墳群。かつては15群・約110基を数えたが、現在では約40基が残る。塚原八十塚と呼ばれ、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に紹介され、古墳研究の先駆けとなった古墳群。 *塚脇古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の古墳群。約50基の古墳で構成されている。 *安満古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の群衆墳。40数基の横穴式石室をもつ古墳が確認されている。 *[[阿武山古墳]] 国の史跡 :7世紀末、古墳時代終末期の古墳。盛り土はなく、直径82mの円墳。[[藤原鎌足]]の墓とされる。 <gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[郡家車塚古墳]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[太田茶臼山古墳]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[今城塚古墳]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[阿武山古墳]] </gallery>
{{DEFAULTSORT:みしまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:茨木市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[三島古墳群]] to [[Mishima Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{出典の明記|date=2013年5月}} '''三島古墳群'''（みしまこふんぐん、三島野古墳群）とは、[[淀川]]北岸、[[桧尾川]]流域から[[茨木川]]流域にあり、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]と[[茨木市]]（旧[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島郡]]）にかけて広がる[[古墳]]と遺跡の総称。

== 主な古墳 == *[[安満宮山古墳]] :3世紀中頃、古墳時代初頭の古墳。三島古墳群最古。 *岡本山古墳 :3世紀後半、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島地方最初の王墓。全長約120m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *弁天山古墳 :3世紀末〜4世紀初頭、古墳時代初期の古墳。三島の王の王墓。全長約100m、後円部径約70mの前方後円墳。 *[[闘鶏山古墳]] 国の史跡 :4世紀前半、古墳時代前期の古墳。全長86.4m、後円部径約60mの前方後円墳。 *[[郡家車塚古墳]] :4世紀末、古墳時代前期の古墳。三島地方の王墓。全長が約85.6m、後円部径約51.3mの前方後円墳。 *[[太田茶臼山古墳]] 継体天皇陵 :5世紀中頃〜後半、古墳時代中期の古墳。総長250m・総幅約150mの前方後円墳。 *[[今城塚古墳]] 国の史跡 :6世紀前半、古墳時代後期の古墳。総長約350m・総幅約340mの前方後円墳。淀川流域最大の墳墓。 *[[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|塚原古墳群]] :6世紀中頃〜7世紀中頃の古墳群。かつては15群・約110基を数えたが、現在では約40基が残る。塚原八十塚と呼ばれ、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に紹介され、古墳研究の先駆けとなった古墳群。 *塚脇古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の古墳群。約50基の古墳で構成されている。 *安満古墳群 :6世紀後半〜7世紀の群衆墳。40数基の横穴式石室をもつ古墳が確認されている。 *[[阿武山古墳]] 国の史跡 :7世紀末、古墳時代終末期の古墳。盛り土はなく、直径82mの円墳。[[藤原鎌足]]の墓とされる。 <gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[郡家車塚古墳]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[太田茶臼山古墳]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[今城塚古墳]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[阿武山古墳]] </gallery>

{{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

}}

* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.

Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

{{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>
{{translated page|ja|三島古墳群|version=101897749|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:大阪府の古墳
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>
{{translated page|ja|三島古墳群|version=101897749|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. * '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.

* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.

A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: sync Kofun‑gun infobox from Wikidata (image/location/coords/etc.)
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Ibaraki|qid=Q502403}}; {{ill|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211948]] Wikidata name: Mishima Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=May 2013}} '''Mishima Kofun Group''' (三島古墳群, Mishima Kofun-gun, also known as Mishimano Kofun Group) refers to the collective name for ancient tombs ('''kofun''') and archaeological sites located along the '''northern bank of the Yodo River''', spanning the '''Hio River Basin''' to the '''Ibaraki River Basin''' in '''Takatsuki City''' and '''Ibaraki City''', Osaka Prefecture (formerly '''Mishima District''').
== Overview == [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The location of the Mishima Kofun Group in relation to other kofun groups]] Located on the '''right bank of the Yodo River''', the kofun group extends from the mountain slopes of the '''Hokusetsu Mountain Range''' to its foothills and consists of over '''500 kofun''' of varying sizes. This region was the center of ancient Mishima Province and contains representative Kofuns from the '''early to final periods of the Kofun era'''. Alongside the [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] near '''Habikino''' and '''Fujiidera''', and the [[Mozu Tombs|Mozu Kofun Group]] in '''Sakai''', the Mishima Kofun Group is one of the largest Kofun clusters in Japan.
== Major Kofun == * '''Amanomiya Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''mid-3rd century''', marking the earliest phase of the Kofun period. The oldest kofun in the Mishima Kofun Group.
* '''Okamotoyama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered the first '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun ('''zenpō-kōen-fun''') measuring approximately '''120 meters''' in total length and '''70 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.
* '''Tokeiyama Kofun''' (闘鶏山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''86.4 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''60 meters'''.
* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. Known as '''Tsukahara Hachijūzuka''', it was introduced to the world by '''William Gowland''', becoming one of the earliest kofun sites in the history of Japanese archaeology.
* '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
* '''Aman Kofun Group''' : A '''clustered kofun site''' from the '''late 6th to 7th century'''. Over '''40 kofun''' with '''yokoana-shiki sekishitsu''' (horizontal stone chamber burial sites) have been identified.
* '''Abuyama Kofun''' (阿武山古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''late 7th century''', in the final phase of the Kofun period. A circular kofun ('''empun''') with no mound, measuring '''82 meters''' in diameter. Thought to be the '''tomb of Fujiwara no Kamatari'''.
<gallery> File:Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun]] File:Oda Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|[[Ōta Chausuyama Kofun]] File:Imashirozuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|[[Imashirozuka Kofun]] File:Abuyama Kofun, boshitsu.jpg|[[Abuyama Kofun]] </gallery>
{{translated page|ja|三島古墳群|version=101897749|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''Benten-yama Kofun''' : A kofun from the '''late 3rd to early 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Thought to be the '''royal tomb''' of the rulers of Mishima. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''100 meters''' and a rear mound diameter of '''70 meters'''.

* '''Gunke Kurumazuka Kofun''' (郡家車塚古墳) : A kofun from the '''late 4th century''', an early Kofun period tomb. Considered a '''royal tomb''' of the Mishima region. A keyhole-shaped kofun measuring approximately '''85.6 meters''' in total length and '''51.3 meters''' in diameter at the rear mound.
* '''Ōta Chausuyama Kofun''' (太田茶臼山古墳) – *Mausoleum of Emperor Keitai* : A kofun from the '''mid-to-late 5th century''', a mid-Kofun period tomb. A keyhole-shaped kofun with a total length of '''250 meters''' and a total width of approximately '''150 meters'''.
* '''Imashirozuka Kofun''' (今城塚古墳) – *National Historic Site* : A kofun from the '''early 6th century''', a late Kofun period tomb. The '''largest Kofun in the Yodo River basin''', measuring approximately '''350 meters''' in total length and '''340 meters''' in total width.
* '''Tsukahara Kofun Group''' (塚原古墳群) : A kofun group dating from the '''mid-6th to mid-7th century'''. Previously contained '''15 subgroups''' with '''about 110 kofun''', but only '''40 kofun''' remain today. * '''Tsukawaki Kofun Group''' : A kofun group from the '''late 6th to 7th century''', consisting of approximately '''50 kofun'''.
